one_line: Gupta emperor who establishes Nalanda as a great mahavihara of learning.

appearance.age_look: Late 30s to early 40s, tall and athletic with a ruler’s upright bearing.
appearance.face: Oval face with sharp jawline, medium-brown North Indian complexion, straight nose, prominent brows, a tilak of red sandal paste on the forehead, and a composed default expression that softens into a slight confident smile.
appearance.hair: Thick black hair tied into a high topknot (kondai-style) at the back of the crown, sides and back neatly trimmed, with a narrow gold circlet resting just above the forehead and a simple central leaf-shaped ornament.
appearance.costume: A knee-length white cotton angarakha (cross-wrapped tunic) with a subtle woven border in dull gold, worn over a deep-red cotton dhoti wrapped in the northern style with pleats in front, a mustard-yellow silk uttariya (shawl) with fine maroon edging draped over the left shoulder and across the torso, a fitted brown leather belt with a simple rectangular bronze buckle at the waist, bare feet with toe-rings on the big toes, a single short gold necklace with a small round pendant, plain gold karnaphuli-style earrings, and a bronze armlet on the right upper arm engraved with a simple geometric pattern.
appearance.signature: A sheathed straight-edged iron sword with a slightly broad blade, simple cross-guard, and plain rounded pommel, hanging from his belt on the left, and in his right hand a rolled palm-leaf manuscript tied with a thin red cord symbolising Nalanda’s foundation charter.

ref_desc: Kumaragupta the First is a tall, athletic, medium-brown man with an oval face, red tilak, black hair in a high topknot under a simple gold circlet, wearing a white angarakha, deep-red dhoti, mustard-yellow shawl, modest gold jewellery, brown belt, and carrying a sheathed straight iron sword at his left hip while holding a rolled palm-leaf manuscript, all in white, deep red, mustard yellow, dull gold, and brown leather tones.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body model sheet of Kumaragupta the First in anime style but with historically grounded Gupta-era North Indian clothing: a tall, athletic man in his late 30s with a medium-brown complexion, oval face, sharp jaw, straight nose, strong brows, and a red sandalwood tilak on his forehead; his thick black hair is tied into a high topknot at the back of the crown, the sides and back neat, and he wears a narrow gold circlet with a small central leaf-shaped ornament resting just above the forehead. Dress him in a knee-length white cotton angarakha with a subtle dull-gold woven border, over a deep-red dhoti wrapped in the northern style with front pleats; drape a mustard-yellow silk uttariya with fine maroon edging over his left shoulder and across his torso. Add a fitted brown leather belt with a plain rectangular bronze buckle around his waist, bare feet with toe-rings on the big toes, a short simple gold necklace with a small round pendant, plain gold earrings, and a bronze armlet with simple geometric engraving on his right upper arm. At his left hip hangs a sheathed straight-edged iron sword with a modest cross-guard and rounded pommel attached to the belt, and in his right hand he holds a rolled palm-leaf manuscript tied with a thin red cord; keep the colour palette dominated by deep red, mustard yellow, white, dull gold, and brown leather, and show front, side, and back views to clearly present his consistent hairstyle, costume, and accessories.
